body:
## Summary
- Refresh issue author associations from the GitHub API when the issue payload reports CONTRIBUTOR.
- Keeps public route policy unchanged while avoiding stale/weaker issue payloads for org members.
- Adds a regression test for issues payload CONTRIBUTOR -> REST MEMBER.

## Verification
- npm --prefix .agent ci
- npm --prefix .agent test

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 77 | PARTIAL | 4 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | fail | 0/7 | PR changes issue-association refresh behavior to include `CONTRIBUTOR` (trusted-without-refresh now only `OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR`) in [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/1a9c22506b1764305e346a5bfd27f79cccd253c5/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts#L33-L116), but docs still describe refresh only for payload `NONE` in [`access-policy.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/access-policy.md#L78-L80). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Change is surgical inside existing `refreshIssueAssociation` flow and existing `ghApi` helper; no duplicate/new parallel mechanism introduced in [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/1a9c22506b1764305e346a5bfd27f79cccd253c5/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts#L97-L116). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR touches only two files aligned with scope: runtime logic and one regression test file (`.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts`, `.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts`). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation is minimal: one local trusted set + normalization/fallback in existing function, plus focused regression test for `CONTRIBUTOR -> MEMBER` in [`extract-context-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/1a9c22506b1764305e346a5bfd27f79cccd253c5/.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts#L125-L178). |

## Notes

- Strong rubric alignment on minimal, focused implementation and regression coverage.
- Main rubric miss is documentation drift: behavior widened beyond what `Issue-body association refresh` currently states.
- No parent/tracking references were present in PR #6 body/comments, so no additional linked-plan context was available to score.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `keep-docs-in-sync` is not satisfied. Update the issue-association refresh docs to reflect that `CONTRIBUTOR` payloads are also refreshed via REST before authorization decisions.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR updates issue-trigger context extraction so `author_association: CONTRIBUTOR` from an `issues` webhook is no longer trusted without refresh.
- The goal is to avoid under-authorizing org members when webhook payload metadata is stale/weaker than the live issue API association.
- Implementation is intentionally small: it narrows the skip-refresh set to `OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR` and keeps using the existing `gh api` refresh path.
- It also adds a regression test for `CONTRIBUTOR` in payload upgrading to `MEMBER` from the API, validating downstream routing behavior.
- No parent/tracking issue references were found in PR #6 discussion/body.

## Review
- Overall direction is correct and the change is surgical, but there are unresolved documentation/test gaps before merge.
- I deduplicated overlapping reviewer feedback and posted one new inline comment for the test-coverage gap not already covered in PR discussion.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Access-policy docs are out of sync with runtime behavior | WARNING | Multiple reviews flagged that refresh behavior now includes payload `CONTRIBUTOR`, while docs still describe the `NONE -> stronger` case only. |
| Missing regression for `CONTRIBUTOR -> CONTRIBUTOR` confirmation path | WARNING | The PR tests `CONTRIBUTOR -> MEMBER`, but not the no-upgrade branch where API confirms `CONTRIBUTOR`; this leaves the always-refresh fallback path unpinned. |

## Progress
- Existing PR discussion already documents the docs-drift concern (rubrics review comment), so no duplicate inline was posted for that.
- I posted one focused inline comment on the new test hunk for the missing symmetric `CONTRIBUTOR -> CONTRIBUTOR` case: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/6#discussion_r3155347431
- No inline comments existed before this run.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Access-policy docs are out of sync with runtime behavior</summary>

**Cause:** Runtime refresh conditions were widened (`CONTRIBUTOR` now refreshed) but [`access-policy.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/access-policy.md) still documents only the `NONE` correction case.

**Candidate solutions:** Update the “Issue-body association refresh” section to describe weaker payload associations such as `NONE` and `CONTRIBUTOR`, and clarify that live API association is used before access decisions.

**Comments:** This is the only finding consistently repeated across reviewer outputs.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Missing regression for `CONTRIBUTOR -> CONTRIBUTOR` confirmation path</summary>

**Cause:** Current added test covers upgrade behavior (`CONTRIBUTOR` payload becoming `MEMBER`) but not the branch where refresh happens and returns the same association.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a test adjacent to the new case in [`extract-context-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/1a9c22506b1764305e346a5bfd27f79cccd253c5/.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts) that mocks issue API returning `CONTRIBUTOR` and asserts stable outputs.

**Comments:** Inline feedback posted at the changed test hunk.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: unresolved findings require concrete branch edits (docs sync + one focused regression test) and are safe for an automated fix pass.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Update `.agent/docs/access-policy.md` to document that issue-event association refresh also applies to payload `CONTRIBUTOR` (not only `NONE`).
- [ ] Add a regression test for the `CONTRIBUTOR` payload case where live issue API also returns `CONTRIBUTOR` and assert expected outputs.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 2/5).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis for PR #6 is `MINOR_ISSUES` with explicit `Recommended Next Step: FIX_PR`, and the remaining findings are concrete, code-safe branch edits.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777390560051 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUwNjIwNzk2ODQ6cmV2aWV3OjY6Zml4X3ByOjY6Mg -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `fix/issue-contributor-refresh`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Updated [`.agent/docs/access-policy.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/access-policy.md) in the **Issue-body association refresh** section to document refresh on weaker payload associations including `CONTRIBUTOR` (not only `NONE`), while noting public-route policy behavior is unchanged.
- Added a focused regression test in [`.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts) for the symmetric `CONTRIBUTOR` payload + refreshed `CONTRIBUTOR` API result, asserting stable expected outputs (`should_respond=true`, `association=CONTRIBUTOR`, `requested_route=answer`).
- Kept changes surgical and limited to the two unresolved review items.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

- `npm --prefix .agent ci` ✅
- `npm --prefix .agent test` ❌ fails during build with `TS5107` from `tsconfig.json` (`moduleResolution=node10` deprecation; requires `ignoreDeprecations: "6.0"` to silence)

</details>

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 3/5).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr completed successfully on PR #6 after a prior review explicitly recommended FIX_PR for MINOR_ISSUES; a follow-up review is needed to confirm both findings are fully resolved.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777390724425 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUwNjI0MzAwMjk6Zml4X3ByOjY6cmV2aWV3OjY6Mw -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 4 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Docs were updated to reflect refresh on weaker issue payload associations including `CONTRIBUTOR` and unchanged public-route policy in [`access-policy.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/732f08031f4dc011e97a2b513be1a870f913addb/.agent/docs/access-policy.md). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The change stays inside existing `refreshIssueAssociation` logic and existing `ghApi` usage; no parallel/new mechanism was introduced in [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/732f08031f4dc011e97a2b513be1a870f913addb/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR scope remains tightly limited to the behavior and its tests/docs: [`.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/732f08031f4dc011e97a2b513be1a870f913addb/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts), [`.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/732f08031f4dc011e97a2b513be1a870f913addb/.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts), and [`.agent/docs/access-policy.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/732f08031f4dc011e97a2b513be1a870f913addb/.agent/docs/access-policy.md). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation is minimal (`ISSUE_ASSOCIATIONS_TRUSTED_WITHOUT_REFRESH` + normalization/fallback), with focused regression coverage for both `CONTRIBUTOR -> MEMBER` and `CONTRIBUTOR -> CONTRIBUTOR` paths in [`extract-context-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/732f08031f4dc011e97a2b513be1a870f913addb/.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts). |

## Notes

- The prior rubric gap (`keep-docs-in-sync`) is now resolved by the docs update in the same PR.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.
- Route/process rubrics (for orchestration/merge/rubric-authoring workflows) were not scored because they are not implementation properties of this code diff.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found in applicable implementation rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR updates issue-context extraction so `issues` webhook payloads reporting `author_association: CONTRIBUTOR` are no longer trusted without refresh.
- Instead, it refreshes weaker payload associations from the GitHub API before authorization routing decisions, while keeping public-route policy behavior unchanged.
- The code change is intentionally narrow: it adjusts the trusted-without-refresh set and keeps using the existing refresh path.
- The PR also adds regression coverage for both `CONTRIBUTOR -> MEMBER` and `CONTRIBUTOR -> CONTRIBUTOR`.
- I checked PR #6 conversation and inline comments; no parent/tracking issue references were present.

## Review
- Across all available reviewer outputs (`claude`, `codex`, and `rubrics`), there are no unresolved actionable defects.
- Prior warnings (docs drift and missing symmetric contributor test) are now addressed, and there are no meaningful reviewer disagreements on merge readiness.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues | INFO | Review artifacts converge on SHIP readiness; remaining notes are non-blocking informational observations. |

## Progress
- Docs were updated to reflect refresh of weaker payload associations (including `CONTRIBUTOR`).
- Symmetric regression coverage (`CONTRIBUTOR -> CONTRIBUTOR`) was added.
- Existing inline comment about missing symmetric coverage is now effectively resolved by the latest diff.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: no unresolved actionable branch-change work remains from the synthesized reviews.

## Final Verdict
`SHIP`

## Action Items
- [ ] None.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #6.

no rubric changes

- Reviewed PR #6 metadata, diff, issue comments, review comments, and reviews.
- Trusted-source check found no maintainer/owner/collaborator preference statements in the PR conversation:
  - Human author/merger `lolipopshock` is `author_association: CONTRIBUTOR` on this PR.
  - All substantive review feedback/comments were from `sepo-agent-app[bot]` with `author_association: NONE` (advisory only).
  - Collaborator admin/maintain lookup returned no additional trusted signals.
- The conversation content was implementation-specific (docs/test gaps) and already aligned with existing active rubrics (especially `keep-docs-in-sync`, `surgical-changes`, `simple-implementation`, `reuse-existing-code`), so there is no new durable, non-duplicate rubric to add.

title: Refresh contributor issue associations
